observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to immediately conduct physical inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the DRRM officer and other concerned officials to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that:The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I of NGAS (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981, in order to safeguard government funds from possible misappropriation and theft; and Further, Report of Collections and Deposits as well as used Official Receipts be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

The municipality must conduct immediately aggressive collection campaign to maximize local revenue. They must implement Section 246, 247, 248, 249, 250 and 251, of the Local Government Code in order to enhance tax collection to hasten economic development.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law as cited above by remitting promptly the employee’s personal contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions and past due accounts like this should be given priority to determine the correct unremitted balances, so that, said account will no longer be carried over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommended to the management to immediately conduct physical inventory
taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances
appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary
adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommend that Municipal Engineer must comply and submit immediately the
necessary complete documents of said projects/equipment and enhance
cooperation and support to the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional
Technical and Information Technology Services project/equipment by providing it
with the complete project requirements.

Likewise the MPDC should strictly follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and
DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that management should direct the procuring department to notify
the Audit Team Leader or any of its representatives on any procurement of supplies,
materials and office equipment consummated for the agency’s operational purposes
during the procuring period within 24 hours after acceptance thereof.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited
intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary
period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I of NGAS (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint
Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981, in order to safeguard government funds from
possible misappropriation and theft; and
b. Further, Report of Collections and Deposits as well as used Official Receipts
be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines
that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the
previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore,
liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law as cited
above by remitting promptly the employee’s personal contributions and deduction in
order not to hamper the benefits that the employee may receive and to avoid penalty
and legal sanctions and past due accounts like this should be given priority to
determine the correct unremitted balances, so that, said account will no longer be
carried over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommend the concerned accountable officers particularly the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the schedule for rendition
of accounts as required for under Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 otherwise known
as the “State Audit Code of the Philippines” and likewise Section 6.03 of COA Cir.
No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA
Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote good
governance through transparency and accountability.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the municipality’s
LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be
submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002. Further, QRF should be transferred from GF to TF books at the end of the
year.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste
Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement
for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to
facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to
establish accountability of the responsible person..

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology Services in the inspection of said project sites. Likewise the MPDC should strictly follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted errors in recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

We recommended to the management to immediately conduct physical inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites. Likewise the MPDC should strictly follow the guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

a. The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I of NGAS (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981, in order to safeguard government funds from possible misappropriation and theft; and
b. Further, Report of Collections and Deposits as well as used Official Receipts be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

The municipality must conduct immediately aggressive collection campaign to maximize local revenue. They must implement Section 246, 247, 248, 249, 250 and 251, of the Local Government Code in order to enhance tax collection to hasten economic development.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law as cited above by remitting promptly the employee’s personal contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions and past due accounts like this should be given priority to determine the correct unremitted balances, so that, said account will no longer be carried over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Further, QRF should be transferred from GF to TF books at the end of the year.

adjustment after establishing the correctness of the balance of the PPE account.

It is mandatory on the part of the management to require the concerned officials to substantiate their claims with proper and complete documentation in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sec. 30-3(d), Chapter VI of SAM. Supporting papers will signify the validity of claims and transactions and will determine fiscal responsibility as stipulated in Sec. 4(4) of PD 1445. Moreover, the completeness of the documents required to support the claim is defined in applicable laws and regulations at times is addressed to the judgments of the concerned officials, including the auditor considering the circumstances obtaining in each case.

   monitoring and measurement of results, and to  establish  accountability
   of the responsible person.

We  have  recommended  that  accountable  officers  must  strictly
     adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance  shall  be
     allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash  advance
     is first settled or proper accounting thereof  is  made.   Furthermore,
     liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the  provisions  of
   law as  cited  above  by  remitting  promptly  the  employee’s  personal
   contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the
   employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions  and  past
   due accounts like this should be given priority to determine the correct
   unremitted balances, so that, said account will  no  longer  be  carried
   over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommend that management observe the general guideline  stipulated
   in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January  30,  2013  RE:  Information  and
   Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of  government  agencies.
   This  is  to  promote   good   governance   through   transparency   and
   accountability.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure  the
   proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate  and  review  the
   implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender  and  Development,
   conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men  and  women,
   discouraging gender  based  violence  between  them  and  encouraging  a
   greater role for men in raising a family.

We  have  recommended  that  the  group  tasked  to  function   as   the
   municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization
   of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph
   5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Further, QRF should  be  transferred
   from GF to TF books at the end of the year.

We  recommended  to  the
management  for  the  completion  of  the  physical  inventory  taking   and
reporting and require the accountant to reconcile the balances appearing  in
the report with that of the  concerned  officer  and  effect  the  necessary
adjustment after establishing the correctness of  the  balance  of  the  PPE
account.
observations.managementAction:
Mgt. reiterated  their  assurance  to  comply  with  the  audit
                                 findings

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Engineer  must  request  for  inspection  of
various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support  to  the
COA  Inspectorate  Team  from  the  Regional   Technical   and   Information
Technology Services in the inspection of said project by providing  it  with
the complete project requirements and  guiding  the  COA  engineers  to  the
project sites.  Likewise the MPDC  should  strictly  follow  the  guidelines
stated  in  the  DILG  and  DBM  Circular  in  programming  the  20%   Local
                         Development Fund.

We recommended  that  the  management  to  support  all  claims  with  the
required documentations prior to release of payments /  checks  to  validate
disbursements.

The  Municipal  Treasurer  should  see  to  it  that  all  collections  be
deposited intact with the nearest  authorized  depository  bank  within  the
reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I  of  NGAS  (LGUs)  and
DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81  dated January 1, 1981,  in  order  to  safeguard
government funds from possible misappropriation and theft; and

The municipality must conduct aggressive collection campaign  to  maximize
local revenue.   They must implement Section 246, 247,  248,  249,  250  and
251, of the Local Government Code in order  to  enhance  tax  collection  to
hasten economic development.

We recommended that the monitoring team should  be  created  to  ensure  the
proper disbursement of the  GAD  Fund,  monitor,  evaluate  and  review  the
implementation of the  Municipality’s  policy  on  Gender  and  Development,
conduct advocacy programs  promoting  partnership  between  men  and  women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and  encouraging  a  greater
role for men in raising a family.

We recommended  to  the  management  to  immediately  conduct  physical
     inventory taking and reporting and require the accountant to  reconcile
     the balances appearing in the report with that of the concerned officer
     and effect the necessary adjustment after establishing the  correctness
     of the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for  inspection  of
     various Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support  to
     the COA Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical  and  Information
     Technology Services in the inspection of said project by  providing  it
     with the complete project requirements and guiding the COA engineers to
     the project sites.   Likewise  the  MPDC  should  strictly  follow  the
     guidelines stated in the DILG and DBM Circular in programming  the  20%
     Local Development Fund.

We  have  recommended  that  accountable  officers  must  strictly
     adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance  shall  be
     allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash  advance
     is first settled or proper accounting thereof  is  made.   Furthermore,
     liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We recommended that the  management  to  support  all  claims  with  the
    required documentations  prior  to  release  of  payments  /  checks  to
    validate disbursements.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the  provisions  of
   law as  cited  above  by  remitting  promptly  the  employee’s  personal
   contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the
   employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions  and  past
   due accounts like this should be given priority to determine the correct
   unremitted balances, so that, said account will  no  longer  be  carried
   over and over again in the financial statement.

We recommended that:

     a.  The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that  all  collections  be
        deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank  within
        the reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol.  I  of  NGAS
        (LGUs) and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981,  in  order
        to safeguard government funds  from  possible  misappropriation  and
        theft; and

     b. Further, Report of Collections and Deposits as well as used Official
        Receipts be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management  be  formalize  and  change
   management strategy in the implementation  of  a  realistic  and  doable
   Solid Waste Management Plan.   Develop  an  Action/Implementation  Plan.
   Draw an arrangement for the  Plan  and  identify  possible  risks  in  a
   comprehensive manner  in  order  to  facilitate  proper  implementation,
   monitoring and measurement of results, and to  establish  accountability
   of the responsible person.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere  to
the provisions of law as cited above by remitting  promptly  the  employee’s
personal contributions and deduction in order not  to  hamper  the  benefits
that the employee may receive and to avoid penalty and legal  sanctions  and
past due accounts like this  should  be  given  priority  to  determine  the
correct unremitted balances,  so  that,  said  account  will  no  longer  be
carried over and over again in the financial statement.

We  noted that instead of accruing 30% or757,969.50 for QRF  to a  special
trust fund account,  the  amount  was  utilized  although  no  calamity  was
declared during the year.  Further,  refrain  from  using  the  30%  of  the
LDRRMF unless a calamity  is  declared.   Transfer  to  special  trust  fund
account the equivalent amount and follow regulation in its utilization.
